summary: Matched much of the MSL math area by replacing asm object references for math_1 and trigf with source-side implementations, adding an MSL math header, and moving frexp/fabsf plus trigonometric symbols into src/MSL. The final diff removes asm/MSL/math_1.s and asm/MSL/trigf.s from the object list, adds src/MSL/math_1.c and expands src/MSL/trigf.c. The trigf work retains high-level NON_MATCHING C for cosf/sinf while using inline asm in the matching build path, so the evidence supports a hybrid decomp/matching step rather than fully cleaned C for every trig function. The PR also includes small newline-only asm cleanups in GX files and an added errors.log/build log file.
what_changed: Deleted asm/MSL/math_1.s and added src/MSL/math_1.c containing frexp and fabsf__Ff.

Deleted asm/MSL/trigf.s from standalone asm and expanded src/MSL/trigf.c with trig constants, tables, cosf/sinf handling, __sinit_trigf_c-related data, NON_MATCHING high-level C, and matching inline asm fallback.
Added src/MSL/math.h with include guard MSL_MATH_H, bit-access macros __HI and __LO, and declarations for frexp, fabsf__Ff, tanf, cos__Ff, sin__Ff, cosf, sinf, __sinit_trigf_c, and func_803265A8.
Updated obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/MSL/math_1.c.o instead of asm/MSL/math_1.s.o and removed asm/MSL/trigf.s.o while keeping src/MSL/trigf.c.o.
Added constants and symbols such as tmp_float, lbl_80400770, lbl_80400774, __four_over_pi_m1, __two_over_pi, lbl_804DE19C, __SQRT_FLT_EPSILON__, and lbl_804DE1A8.
Made newline-at-end-of-file cleanup changes in asm/dolphin/gx/GXInit.s and asm/dolphin/gx/GXStubs.s.
Added errors.log containing a local build log with NON_MATCHING=0, GENERATE_MAP=1, EPILOGUE_PROCESS=1, and build OK output.
smart_moves: Moved object linkage in obj_files.mk at the same time as deleting asm files, which made the source-side math_1/trigf work participate in the normal build instead of leaving stale asm objects linked.
Introduced a dedicated MSL math header so shared math symbols and prototypes could be included explicitly by source files instead of relying on ad hoc declarations.
For frexp, used __HI and __LO macros to express the original double bit manipulation directly in C, preserving the exponent/significand behavior visible in the deleted assembly.
Kept trig polynomial/range-reduction constants as named source symbols, making the cosf/sinf implementation more searchable than raw lbl_ addresses alone.

Preserved matching behavior for difficult trig functions with inline asm while keeping NON_MATCHING C documentation/attempts nearby, which can be useful when an exact high-level match is not yet ready.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling library math routines, bit-level macros like __HI(x) and __LO(x) can be necessary to match fdlibm/MSL-style implementations such as frexp.
Source migration is not complete until obj_files.mk is switched from asm objects to src objects; otherwise the new C may compile but not be linked into the target object set.

For difficult float math, preserve exact constants and FPU operation order. The trigf code relies on range reduction constants, polynomial tables, fctiwz-style integer conversion behavior, and fmadds/fnmadds sequences.
A practical intermediate state is to keep NON_MATCHING readable C and a matching inline asm body under #else, but future work should not mistake that for a fully matched high-level C decomp.
Use explicit project include paths for shared headers. Review specifically requested replacing a local relative include like "./math.h" with an explicit MSL path.

Committing generated build logs such as errors.log may help show build context but is usually noisy for decomp history; this PR contains one, but the context does not show reviewer discussion about it.
naming_conventions: Project include style preferred by review: use an explicit MSL path such as <MSL/math.h> or "MSL/math.h" instead of "./math.h".
MSL C++-mangled wrapper-style symbols were kept where present: fabsf__Ff, cos__Ff, sin__Ff.
Known or inferred math constants were given semantic names where possible: __four_over_pi_m1, __two_over_pi, __SQRT_FLT_EPSILON__, __sincos_poly, __sincos_on_quadrant.
Some raw lbl_ names remained for data whose semantics were not fully renamed: lbl_80400770, lbl_80400774, lbl_804DE19C, lbl_804DE1A8, lbl_804DE190.
Unmatched or not-yet-understood function name func_803265A8 remained in the header and PR checklist rather than being guessed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted standalone asm files only after moving equivalent symbols into src/MSL and updating obj_files.mk.
Used inline asm inside src/MSL/trigf.c for the matching build path while placing high-level C under #ifdef NON_MATCHING.

Preserved PowerPC floating-point details in trig routines, including fctiwz conversion, paired stack stores/loads for integer extraction, fmadds/fnmadds polynomial evaluation, and exact quadrant indexing.
Used the 0x43300000/0x80000000 double conversion constant represented as lbl_804DE1A8 to reproduce int-to-float conversion behavior.
Used __HI/__LO double word access in frexp to match exponent and mantissa manipulation, including subnormal scaling by lbl_804DE190 and exponent adjustment by -54.
Retained exact data layout symbols for constants/tables so relocations and sdata2/data placement could continue to match.

follow_up_queries: Check whether func_803265A8 was later matched and whether its declaration/name changed after PR #235.
Search PR #241 for labeling work related to MSL/math constants and trig functions.
Audit whether errors.log remained in the repository or was later removed as generated build noise.
Find later PRs that replace the inline asm cosf/sinf bodies in src/MSL/trigf.c with fully matching high-level C.
Verify whether raw lbl_80400770/lbl_80400774/lbl_804DE19C/lbl_804DE1A8 names received semantic names in later MSL math cleanup.
